comment: LEMUR NAS — Blip2Fast Visual Projection Optimization (Direct Replacement)
LLM must output the CrossModalBridge class in <nn_head> tags.
The script will automatically inject this class into the baseline and replace the default nn.Linear projection.
improvement_captioning_delta.description: Visual Projection Optimization via Direct Code Injection for Blip2Fast.
system: You are an expert in PyTorch and multimodal architectures.
You output ONLY valid, self-contained PyTorch classes.
Your generated class will be directly injected into the codebase.
keep_same: OPT model weights (frozen, do not modify)
prompt: You are a NAS engineer optimizing a Blip2Fast image captioning model.
Your ONLY task is to write a single PyTorch class named `CrossModalBridge`.
Write a `CrossModalBridge(nn.Module)` class to replace the baseline's simple linear visual projection.
The bridge sits between a frozen Q-Former and a frozen OPT-2.7B decoder.
- `__init__(self, prm, opt_embed_dim)`: You must accept these two arguments.
- `forward(self, visual_features)`: visual_features has shape (B, 32, 768) — dtype float32.
- The forward method MUST return a tensor of shape (B, 32, opt_embed_dim) — EXACTLY 3D.
- QFORMER_HIDDEN = 768. The input dimension is always 768.
- The output dimension must always be `opt_embed_dim` (which is passed in __init__).
- Do NOT apply `.to(device)` inside the class (the external script handles this).
- Do NOT import anything. torch, nn, F are already available.
- Do NOT write anything outside the class.
## WORKING EXAMPLE — Study this format carefully
The example below uses a depth-wise MLP bottleneck:
## INNOVATION IDEAS (pick ONE approach different from the example)
- Gated projection: learn a sigmoid gate to blend linear and nonlinear paths.
- Attention pooling: use nn.MultiheadAttention to let visual tokens attend to each other before projection (make sure embed_dim is divisible by num_heads).
- Token mixing: apply a small transformer encoder layer before the final linear projection.
# ... your entire valid python code here ...
CRITICAL: Output ONLY <hp> and <nn_head> tags. No explanations. No markdown.
